recentActivity.reviewText:
pretty great

Zootopia is for everyone

recentActivity.title: Heroic Times, 1983 - ★★★½
recentActivity.reviewText: wow, the animation here is incredible. it's sometimes what i expect and at other times it surprised me. i felt like the score and the writing were both... bleh. don't even get me started about the boring-ass voice actor narrating the whole thing. cool movie, though. fun watch.

recentActivity.title: Autism Is a World, 2004 - ★
recentActivity.reviewText: 2004 was a long time ago and bad coverage is still coverage, but woof this is wrong-headed in so many ways that can only fit the zeitgeist of the naughts.

recentActivity.title: The Librarians, 2025 - ★★★★★
recentActivity.reviewText: nearly had me weeping in a theater full of people

recentActivity.title: Ex Machina, 2015 - ★★★★★
recentActivity.reviewText:
I hosted a round of Tech Movie Trivia featuring Ex Machina.

Answer 10 questions in 10 minutes.

recentActivity.title: Kimi
recentActivity.reviewText: as connected as we all are, it feels like there is such a thing as too much connection. this film captures the intensity of that from a specific perspective, and i appreciate the immersive experience offered.

recentActivity.title: Sinners
recentActivity.reviewText: Sammie at the edge of his life said “that was the best day of my life” and he was reckoning with the night that followed, probably all this time.  I think the Stack mid-credits ending means Sammie went to his grave with some kind of peace about the events of October 16, 1932. I truly believe so.

recentActivity.filmTitle: You Got Gold: A Celebration of John Prine
recentActivity.title: You Got Gold: A Celebration of John Prine
recentActivity.reviewText:
I am grateful that the name of the person speaking always appeared next to them, even if it wasn’t the first time they appeared on screen. It was helpful and now I know I love Bonnie Raitt, Tyler Childers, and Brandi Carlile 

Thanks Mississippi Film Society for this screening!

recentActivity.title: Cool Hand Luke
recentActivity.reviewText: a second watch with commentary from Eric Lax

recentActivity.title: Cool Hand Luke
recentActivity.reviewText: i'm convinced that Ryan Coogler referenced this film in Sinners with the twins hiding their stash in a truck hidden by brush (plus the rattlesnake, though that's a separate sequence that Coogler likely combined for an authorial statement) this is so theologically potent, I remember why I watched this in bible college this is the first film i've borrowed from the Willie Morris library

recentActivity.title: The Virgin Suicides
recentActivity.reviewText: "Why am I crying? Will I accidentally listen to 'Come Sail Away' by Styx again?"

lists.name: My 2024 Releases Ranked
lists.description: Updated January 1

lists.name: A24 Ranked
lists.description: adding the entire filmography to this list today (march 30) but will need to come back when i have more time to rank everything.

lists.name: 2022 Goal: 100 New Directors
lists.description: I started the year with a pretty interesting ratio of 1.28:1 for number of films by a director. On 14 October 2021 I reached a milestone of 500 directors from 646 logged films, spanning 41 countries, inspiring my 2022 goal of watching 100 movies introducing myself to that many more new directors. As of completion of this goal, my ratio is 1.33 movies per director with over 700 directors logged. I look forward to seeing where it's at when I hit 1500 movies logged

lists.description: Updated as of 1 January 2024

lists.description: I attended the 2024 Oxford Film Festival and got the opportunity to interview a number of the filmmakers for Jackson Film Club. You'll get the chance to see those soon!

lists.description: Quentin Jones, friend of the film club, listed his top 20 on the most recent episode of Jackson Film Club The Podcast.

lists.name: JXNFC: Ben Wilson's Top 20
lists.description: Ben's list is in no particular order and includes five films deserving honorable mention. Classics, pop culture phenomenons, indie darlings, thought-provoking cinematic features, buddy comedy. This list has it all.

lists.description: <blockquote>These are the films that have influenced me throughout my life, from the ages of 0 to today. I consider them to be my most rewatchable films of all time. I’m sure this list will change, but this is it right now.

lists.name: JXNFC: Michael's Top 20 (2025 REDUX)
lists.description: Five years ago, I set down my Top 20 Favorites and then started a podcast where I collected other people’s Top 20s and had them talk about it on the internet. This is an updated list, not invalidating the previous Top 20, just memorializing my current Top 20.

lists.name: Bear's November Movie Challenge
lists.description: Bear challenged me to list movies with a "November" vibe. Bear gave a subjective trifecta but I've forgotten it (please add it in a comment, thank you!)

lists.name: JXNFC: Brennan White’s Top 20
lists.description: The top 20 films that I enjoy watching, not necessarily what I believe to be the “best” movies made.

lists.name: JXNFC: Jonathan Miles’ Top 20 Films
lists.description: Classics, action, comedy, fantasy. This list has a little of everything.

lists.name: JXNFC: Mary Heath's Top 20

lists.name: JXNFC: Michael Lamb's Top 20 (2020)

lists.name: JXNFC: Michael Tran's 20 Interesting Films
lists.description: Michael Tran is my manager at C Spire. We chat about movies. Here's a list he thought worth sharing with me.

- Sam
